data.title: Significant Liquidation of Short Positions Following Bullish CPI Report
data.description: Crypto KOL Ted posted on X that $150 million in short positions have been liquidated in the past three hours. This development follows a bullish Consumer Price Index (CPI) report, which Ted suggested would lead to a hunt for short positions.

data.title: Tri-Party Custody Model Launched for Institutional SOL Staking
data.description: Anchorage Digital, Kamino, and Solana Company have partnered to launch a tri-party custody model aimed at institutional SOL staking. According to NS3.AI, this innovative framework allows institutions to engage in compliant staking while ensuring assets remain within regulated custody. The model facilitates access to on-chain lending and liquidity services without necessitating asset transfers outside the custody environment.

data.title: Hybrid Mortgage Loans Combine Bitcoin and Real Estate for Credit Access
data.description: Newmarket Capital is introducing a novel approach to mortgage loans by integrating Bitcoin and real estate as collateral. According to NS3.AI, this initiative aims to offer traditional credit access to borrowers without necessitating the liquidation of their cryptocurrency holdings. The strategy is designed to appeal to crypto-savvy individuals by combining income-generating properties with Bitcoin, thereby mitigating volatility risks while enabling participation in Bitcoin's potential appreciation. Although regulatory frameworks mandate that Bitcoin must be held on regulated exchanges for mortgage qualification, this development represents a significant advancement towards the mainstream adoption of cryptocurrency in real-world lending.

data.title: Altcoins Exhibit Mixed Performance Amid Volatile Market Conditions
data.description: Altcoins are displaying varied performance this week, with certain assets experiencing significant rallies while others encounter declines. According to NS3.AI, Pippin (PIPPIN) has surged over 200%, indicating breakout potential. Meanwhile, Aptos (APT) remains oversold and near its lows, and Kite (KITE) continues to reach new highs with strong momentum. Investors are advised to closely monitor support and resistance levels to effectively manage risk in these volatile conditions.

data.description:

data.title: Scott Melker Predicts $COIN's Rise Amid Earnings Report
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X, expressing optimism about $COIN's future. He noted that despite negative earnings perceptions, the company is poised to become one of the top 10 globally. $COIN is currently trading near its two-year low, presenting a potential buying opportunity according to Melker. He highlighted the weekly candle at support as a positive indicator and shared that he has increased his holdings.

data.title: U.S. Health Officials Jim O'Neill and Mike Stewart to Step Down
data.description: Jim O'Neill, the Deputy Secretary of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, along with General Counsel Mike Stewart, are set to resign from their current positions. According to Jin10, this development comes amid ongoing changes within the department. The reasons for their departure have not been disclosed, and it remains unclear who will succeed them in their roles. This transition is part of a broader reshuffle within the U.S. health administration.

data.title: The U.S. Supreme Court will issue its opinion on the Trump tariff case on February 20.
data.description: The U.S. Supreme Court has set February 20 as the next date for issuing its opinion, with the world awaiting a ruling that could largely nullify President Trump's signature tariff policies. Reports indicate that the justices are also expected to issue their opinions on February 24 and 25. This tariff dispute is one of 12 cases debated in October or November that have yet to be decided. U.S. federal government data shows that the disputed tariffs cost importers over $16 billion per month. Economic analyst Chris Kennedy calculates that at this rate, by February 20, the total amount of taxes collected under the core law of the case, the International Emergency Economic Powers Act of 1977, could exceed $170 billion. (Jinshi)

data.title: US cryptocurrency stocks continued their upward trend, with Coinbase's gains widening to 15.1%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, the US cryptocurrency sector continued its upward trend, with Coinbase rising by 15.1%, Robinhood by 7.09%, and Strategy by 7.69%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stocks and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Trump advisor Epshteyn and others are facing a class-action lawsuit from investors for promoting the Meme coin "Let's Go Brandon".
data.description: Trump advisor Epshteyn and others are facing a class-action lawsuit from investors over their promotion of the Meme coin "Let's Go Brandon." The lawsuit, filed in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia, accuses the individuals of knowingly making false claims about the coin's decentralization, while in reality, the operators had control over smart contracts, governance, and transactions, including the power to freeze accounts. (The Block)

data.title: Crypto's Gambling Era Ends, Calls for Portfolio Approach
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X, stating that the era of treating crypto as gambling is over. He emphasizes the need to view digital assets as a portfolio rather than a slot machine.

data.title: SEC Chair Atkins Criticizes Regulatory Approach
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X, highlighting SEC Chair Atkins' statement. Atkins emphasized that the SEC should focus on enforcing existing laws rather than regulating through enforcement actions.

data.title: Russia's January CPI Rises Below Expectations
data.description: Russia's Consumer Price Index (CPI) for January increased by 1.62% month-on-month, falling short of the anticipated 2.00% rise. According to Jin10, the year-on-year CPI growth was recorded at 6.00%, also below the forecasted 6.45%. Additionally, the core CPI, which excludes volatile items, rose by 0.95% month-on-month and 5.43% year-on-year. These figures indicate a slower inflation rate than expected, potentially impacting economic forecasts and monetary policy decisions.

data.title: Bitcoin Approaches Key Structural Inflection After Sharp Pullback
data.description:
Bitcoin is once again testing a decisive structural zone after a sharp rejection from resistance. The broader technical framework now shows price compressing between long-term support and a descending trendline, setting up a pivotal moment for the next directional move.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-approaches-key-structural-inflection-after-sharp-pullback/

data.title: TSA to Refer to Non-U.S. Citizens as 'Aliens' in Regulations
data.description: The U.S. Transportation Security Administration (TSA) has announced a regulatory change that will see non-U.S. citizens referred to as 'aliens.' Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this update, which was revealed on Friday. This change aligns with terminology used in other federal regulations and aims to standardize language across government agencies. The TSA's decision reflects ongoing adjustments in regulatory language to ensure consistency and clarity in official documents.

data.title: Security Concerns Escalate in Southern Nigeria
data.description: Nigeria is experiencing a shift in its security crisis, with threats from jihadists and armed gangs moving toward the commercial capital. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing concerns as violence spreads to areas previously unaffected by decades of turmoil. The situation poses significant challenges for the nation's stability and economic prospects, as authorities grapple with the expanding reach of these groups. Efforts to address the security threats are becoming increasingly urgent as the crisis impacts more regions.

data.title: Indian National Admits Role in 2023 Plot Against Sikh Activist in New York
data.description: An Indian national has confessed to participating in a thwarted 2023 scheme to assassinate a Sikh activist holding U.S. citizenship in New York. Bloomberg posted on X, detailing the individual's admission of involvement in the criminal conspiracy. The plot, which targeted the activist, was uncovered before it could be executed, leading to the arrest and subsequent confession of the accused. The case highlights ongoing concerns about targeted violence against activists and the international dimensions of such plots. Further details about the investigation and potential legal proceedings are yet to be disclosed.

data.title: FedEx Joins Hedera Council to Enhance Global Supply Chain Digitalization
data.description: FedEx has become a member of the Hedera Council, a move aimed at advancing the digital transformation of global supply chains. According to NS3.AI, the company is focusing on the importance of trusted and decentralized data sharing to facilitate efficient commerce without increasing risk or centralization. Despite some recent gains, Hedera's HBAR token continues to trade well below its all-time high.

data.title: U.S. Inflation Unaffected by Tariffs, Says Economist
data.description: Economist Tani Fukui from MetLife has reported that U.S. inflation remains largely unaffected by tariffs. According to Jin10, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) for January was 2.4%, moving closer to the Federal Reserve's 2% inflation target after December's 2.7% reading. Fukui described the inflation report as benign, supporting the view that the impact of tariffs this year will be minimal. She noted that most commodity prices have seen moderate increases. Fukui maintains her prediction of three interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve this year, citing greater concerns about the labor market than inflation.
